name: per_addr_crash_determinism_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_per_addr_crash_determinism_external_body(
    pm_region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    s: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr < pm_region_view.len(),
        pm_region_view.state[addr].outstanding_write.is_none(),
        pm_region_view.can_crash_as(s),
    ensures
        s[addr] == pm_region_view.committed()[addr],
{
    lemma_if_no_outstanding_writes_at_addr_then_persistent_memory_view_can_only_crash_as_committed(pm_region_view, addr);
}
reason: `lemma_if_no_outstanding_writes_at_addr_then_persistent_memory_view_can_only_crash_as_committed` is `external_body` — the per-address crash determinism when no outstanding write exists is trusted without proof; if a chunk containing this byte crashes as flushed but the byte has no write, it should still equal committed, but this reasoning is unverified

name: crash_as_admits_all_committed
code:
proof fn phi_2_crash_as_admits_all_committed(view: PersistentMemoryRegionView)
    requires
        forall|k: int| 0 <= k < view.len() ==> (#[trigger] view.state[k].outstanding_write).is_none(),
    ensures
        view.can_crash_as(view.committed()),
{
}
reason: When there are no outstanding writes, the committed state should be a valid crash outcome — if `can_crash_as` rejected committed, no valid crash state could exist

name: crash_as_admits_flushed_state
reason: The fully-flushed state should always be a valid crash outcome — every chunk satisfies `chunk_corresponds_after_flush`; if the flushed state weren't admitted, the crash model would reject a physically realizable outcome

name: two_addrs_same_chunk_same_outcome
code:
proof fn phi_4_two_addrs_same_chunk_same_outcome(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    s: Seq<u8>,
    a: int,
    b: int,
)
    requires
        view.can_crash_as(s),
        0 <= a < view.len(),
        0 <= b < view.len(),
        a / const_persistence_chunk_size() == b / const_persistence_chunk_size(),
        view.state[a].outstanding_write.is_none(),
        view.state[b].outstanding_write.is_none(),
    ensures
        s[a] == view.state[a].state_at_last_flush,
        s[b] == view.state[b].state_at_last_flush,
{
    lemma_if_no_outstanding_writes_at_addr_then_persistent_memory_view_can_only_crash_as_committed(view, a);
    lemma_if_no_outstanding_writes_at_addr_then_persistent_memory_view_can_only_crash_as_committed(view, b);
}
reason: Two addresses in the same chunk with no outstanding writes must both crash as committed — this is a corollary of the per-address external_body lemma, testing that the trusted assumption composes correctly across addresses

name: mixed_chunk_outstanding_write_addr_can_differ
reason: A chunk with mixed clean/dirty bytes can crash as flushed — the dirty byte takes its outstanding write value while the clean byte's flush_byte equals state_at_last_flush anyway; tests that the per-chunk model correctly handles heterogeneous outstanding writes within a chunk
